fields.title: American Cheeseburger
fields.description:
1) Preparation of the meat:
   - Use ground beef with a fat content of at least 20% (this is key for juiciness). Keep the meat cold until the moment of cooking.
   - Divide the meat into 4 equal parts (150g each). Gently form round balls, but do not pack them too tightly to keep the texture tender.
   - Flatten the balls into patties slightly wider than your buns (meat shrinks during cooking). Make a small indentation in the center with your thumb to prevent the patty from bulging.
   - Generously season with salt and pepper ONLY on the outside, right before frying.

2) Toasting the buns:
   - Cut the buns in half. Lightly butter the cut sides.
   - Fry them on a dry pan or grill until golden brown (30-60 seconds). This creates a barrier so the sauce doesn't make the bun soggy.

3) Frying the patties:
   - Heat a cast-iron skillet or grill to high heat. Add a drop of oil.
   - Place the patties and fry for 3-4 minutes on one side until a dark, caramelized crust forms (Maillard reaction).
   - Flip the patties. Immediately place a slice of cheese on each patty.
   - Cover with a lid for 1 minute to melt the cheese perfectly.

4) Assembly (Classic order):
   - Bottom bun -> Sauce (mix ketchup, mayonnaise, and mustard) -> Lettuce (acts as a shield for the juice) -> Patty with melted cheese -> Tomato slices -> Onion rings -> Pickles -> Top bun with sauce.
   - Press slightly to bond the ingredients.

5) Serving:
   - Serve immediately with fries or coleslaw. The burger should be juicy, with the sauce dripping slightly.

fields.title: NY Style Hot Dog
fields.description:
1) The Onions (The secret weapon):
   - Finely dice the onions or slice them into half-rings.
   - Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add onions and sauté slowly for 10-15 minutes.
   - When onions are soft and golden, add a pinch of sugar (or ketchup) and a splash of water to create a 'saucy' consistency. This is the classic New York pushcart onion sauce.

2) The Sausages:
   - For an authentic taste, sausages should be grilled or boiled. If grilling: cook over medium heat until they have grill marks and are heated through.
   - If boiling (dirty water dog style): simmer gently in water seasoned with a little vinegar and spices for 5-7 minutes.

3) The Buns:
   - Steam the buns for 1-2 minutes until they are soft and warm (you can place a colander over a pot of boiling water).
   - Alternatively, toast them lightly inside for texture.

4) Assembly:
   - Place the sausage in the warm bun.
   - Apply a zig-zag of yellow mustard (classic choice).
   - Top generously with the caramelized onion mixture.
   - Add ketchup if desired (though purists might argue against it!).
   - Optional: Add sauerkraut if available for extra tang.

5) Serve immediately wrapped in foil or paper to keep everything warm and soft.

fields.title: Creamy Coleslaw
fields.description:
1) Vegetable Prep:
   - Remove the outer damaged leaves of the cabbage. Cut into quarters and remove the hard core.
   - Shred the cabbage very finely using a sharp knife or a mandoline slicer. The thinner the better for texture.
   - Grate the carrots on the large holes of a box grater.
   - Finely chop a small amount of onion (optional, for bite).
   - Combine vegetables in a large bowl.

2) The Dressing:
   - In a separate small bowl, whisk together mayonnaise, vinegar (apple cider or white), sugar, salt, and black pepper.
   - Taste the dressing: it should be a balance of creamy, sweet, and tangy. Adjust vinegar or sugar to your preference.

3) Combining:
   - Pour 2/3 of the dressing over the vegetables.
   - Toss well to coat. The cabbage will release water over time, so start with less dressing than you think you need.
   - If it looks too dry, add the remaining dressing.

4) Marinating (Crucial Step):
   - Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour (preferably 2-3 hours). This allows the cabbage to soften slightly and absorb the flavors.
   - Stir again before serving and drain any excess liquid if it's too watery.

5) Serving:
   - Perfect side dish for BBQ, burgers (Recipe 51), or fried chicken. It cuts through the fat of heavy meats.

fields.title: Classic American Meatloaf
fields.description:
1) The Base:
   - Preheat oven to 190°C.
   - Finely dice the onion and mince the garlic. Sauté them in a little oil until soft (5 mins) and let cool. This prevents crunchy raw onions in the soft meatloaf.
   - In a large bowl, whisk the egg with salt, pepper, and dried herbs (if using).
   - Add breadcrumbs (or milk-soaked bread) to the egg mixture. This acts as a binder (panade) to keep the meat moist.

2) Mixing:
   - Add the ground beef and the cooled onion mixture to the bowl.
   - Add a tablespoon of mustard and ketchup for internal flavor.
   - Mix gently with your hands. DO NOT overmix, or the meatloaf will be tough and rubbery. Stop as soon as it's combined.

3) Shaping:
   - Transfer the mixture to a baking sheet lined with foil or parchment.
   - Shape it into a loaf (approx. 10x20 cm). Using a free-form shape on a sheet creates a better crust than using a loaf pan.

4) The Glaze (The best part):
   - In a small bowl, mix ketchup, a teaspoon of mustard, and brown sugar (or regular sugar).
   - Brush half of this glaze over the entire loaf before baking.

5) Baking:
   - Bake for 45-55 minutes. Halfway through cooking, brush with the remaining glaze for a sticky, caramelized top.
   - Internal temperature should reach 70°C.
   - Let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing, otherwise, it will crumble.

6) Serve with mashed potatoes and green beans.

fields.title: Sticky Honey-Soy Chicken Wings
fields.description:
1) Wing Prep:
   - Pat the chicken wings very dry with paper towels. Moisture is the enemy of crispiness.
   - If using whole wings, separate them into drums and flats; discard tips.
   - Toss wings with a little oil, salt, and pepper.

2) Baking (Crispy method):
   - Preheat oven to 200°C.
   - Arrange wings in a single layer on a wire rack over a baking sheet (this allows air to circulate underneath).
   - Bake for 45-50 minutes, flipping once halfway through, until skin is crispy and golden brown.

3) The Sauce:
   - While wings bake, combine soy sauce, honey, ketchup, minced garlic, and vinegar in a small saucepan.
   - Bring to a simmer over medium heat.
   - Cook for 5-7 minutes until the sauce reduces and thickens into a glaze that coats the back of a spoon.
   - Taste: it should be savory (soy), sweet (honey), and tangy (vinegar/ketchup).

4) Glazing:
   - Transfer the hot, crispy wings to a large bowl.
   - Pour the hot glaze over them.
   - Toss vigorously until every wing is coated in sticky sauce.

5) Serving:
   - Serve immediately while hot and sticky.
   - garnish with sesame seeds or green onions if available.
   - Keep plenty of napkins nearby!

fields.title: Tex-Mex Chili con Carne
fields.description:
1) Prep:
   - Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic.
   - Drain the canned beans and corn slightly (you can keep a little liquid for body).
   - This is a 'dump and simmer' meal, perfect for a hearty dinner.

2) Browning the Meat:
   - Heat oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high heat.
   - Add ground beef and cook, breaking it up with a wooden spoon, until brown and crispy bits form (about 8-10 mins). Don't rush this—browning equals flavor.
   - Add the chopped onions and cook for another 5 minutes until soft.
   - Add minced garlic and chili flakes (adjust spice level to taste) and stir for 30 seconds until fragrant.

3) The Simmer:
   - Pour in the canned tomatoes (with their juices). Crush them with the spoon if they are whole.
   - Add the canned beans and canned corn.
   - Season with salt, plenty of black pepper, and if you have any dried oregano or cumin in your pantry (even if not in the list), throw it in.
   - Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low.

4) Cooking:
   - Cover slightly askew and simmer for at least 30-40 minutes.
   - The liquid should reduce and thicken into a rich sauce.
   - If it gets too thick, add a splash of water. If too thin, simmer uncovered for the last 10 minutes.

5) Serving:
   - Serve hot in bowls.
   - It pairs perfectly with rice or just crusty bread.

fields.title: American-Chinese Fried Rice
fields.description:
1) The Rice Rule:
   - Use COLD, leftover rice. If you use freshly boiled hot rice, it will turn into mush. Cook the rice the day before or spread fresh rice on a tray and freeze for 20 mins to dry it out.

2) Prep:
   - Beat the eggs in a small bowl with a pinch of salt.
   - Drain canned corn and peas.
   - Finely dice the onion and slice the green onions (keep white and green parts separate).

3) The Scramble:
   - Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a wok or large pan over high heat.
   - Pour in the eggs and scramble quickly until just set but still soft. Remove eggs from the pan and set aside.

4) The Fry:
   - Add more oil to the hot pan.
   - Add onions and the white parts of green onions. Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
   - Add the cold rice. Break up any clumps with a spatula.
   - Fry the rice undisturbed for 1-2 minutes to let it get slightly crispy, then toss.
   - Add corn and peas.

5) Seasoning:
   - Drizzle soy sauce over the rice (not in one spot, but around the pan). The rice should turn a light golden-brown color.
   - Add the cooked eggs back in and break them up into smaller pieces.
   - Toss everything together for another 2 minutes.
   - Finish with the green parts of the green onion.

6) Serve immediately as a main dish or side.

fields.title: Sausage & Rice Skillet
fields.description:
1) Prep:
   - Slice the sausages into rounds (coins).
   - Dice the bell pepper and onion.
   - Rinse the rice under cold water until clear to remove excess starch.

2) Searing:
   - Heat a deep skillet over medium-high heat. Add the sausage slices.
   - Fry until they are browned and have released some of their flavorful fat. Remove sausage to a plate, leaving the fat in the pan.

3) The Base:
   - Add onion and bell pepper to the sausage fat (add a little oil if dry).
   - Sauté for 5-7 minutes until vegetables are softened and slightly charred.
   - Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.

4) Toasting & Simmering:
   - Add the raw rice to the pan and stir to coat it in the oils. Toasting the rice for 1 minute adds a nutty flavor.
   - Pour in the canned tomatoes (with juice) and water (ratio: 2 parts liquid to 1 part rice).
   - Return the sausages to the pan.
   - Bring to a boil, then cover with a tight lid.

5) Cooking:
   - Reduce heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes. DO NOT peek or stir, or the steam will escape.
   - Turn off the heat and let it stand covered for another 5-10 minutes. This ensures the rice is fluffy.

6) Fluff with a fork and serve.

fields.title: Honey Mustard Glazed Chicken
fields.description:
1) The Sauce (Marinade & Glaze):
   - In a bowl, whisk together the mustard (this gives the tang), honey (sweetness), and mayonnaise (creaminess and coating).
   - Ideally, use a ratio of 2 parts mustard, 2 parts honey, and 1 part mayo.
   - Add a pinch of salt and black pepper.
   - Reserve half of this sauce for serving later.

2) Chicken Prep:
   - Cut chicken breasts into large bite-sized chunks or leave whole if preferred (cooking time will vary).
   - Toss the chicken in the remaining half of the sauce to coat evenly.

3) Cooking:
   - Heat oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat.
   - Add the chicken pieces. Do not overcrowd the pan.
   - Cook for 5-7 minutes per side. The honey in the sauce will caramelize quickly, creating a dark, sticky crust. Watch carefully so it doesn't burn black.
   - If the sauce browns too fast, lower the heat.

4) Resting:
   - Remove chicken from the pan and let it rest for 5 minutes.

5) Serving:
   - Serve the chicken over steamed rice (to use up your rice stock).
   - Drizzle the reserved fresh Honey-Mustard sauce over the top for an extra kick.
   - A simple and kid-friendly American classic.

fields.title: Creamy Tuna & Rice Salad
fields.description:
1) Rice Base:
   - Cook the rice in salted water until tender. Drain and rinse under cold water to stop the cooking and cool it down rapidly.
   - Shake well to remove excess water (watery salad is bad).

2) Prep Mix-ins:
   - Open and drain the canned tuna thoroughly. Flake it with a fork into chunks.
   - Drain the canned corn.
   - Dice the cucumber into small cubes for crunch.
   - Finely chop the green onions.

3) The Dressing:
   - In a large mixing bowl, combine a generous amount of mayonnaise with a splash of lemon juice (if available) or vinegar.
   - Season with salt and lots of black pepper.

4) Assembly:
   - Add the cold rice, tuna, corn, cucumber, and green onions to the bowl with the dressing.
   - Fold everything together gently until well combined.

5) chilling:
   - This salad tastes best after sitting in the fridge for 30 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the rice to firm up slightly.

6) Serving:
   - Perfect for lunchboxes, picnics, or a light dinner.
   - It's a great way to turn pantry staples (canned goods + rice) into a complete meal.

fields.title: Healthy Turkey Burger
fields.description:
1) The Meat Mix:
   - Turkey meat is leaner than beef, so it needs help to stay juicy. In a bowl, mix the ground turkey with salt, plenty of black pepper, and a tablespoon of mayonnaise (this is the secret to moisture).
   - Gently form into 4 patties. Don't overwork the meat. Press a small dimple in the center of each patty.

2) Cooking:
   - Heat oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat (turkey burns faster than beef, so avoid high heat).
   - Cook patties for 5-6 minutes per side. Unlike beef, turkey must be cooked through completely (no pink centers).

3) Assembly:
   - Lightly toast the buns.
   - Spread a little more mayo or your favorite sauce on the bun.
   - Layer lettuce, a slice of tomato, and the turkey patty.
   - Top with raw or caramelized onions.

4) Serving:
   - Great with a side salad or baked sweet potato fries for a lighter American meal.

fields.title: Buffalo Cauliflower Wings
fields.description:
1) The Batter:
   - Preheat oven to 220°C.
   - Cut the cauliflower head into bite-sized florets.
   - In a bowl, whisk flour, milk (or water), salt, and garlic powder (if you have it, otherwise minced garlic) to create a batter. It should be thick enough to coat a spoon.

2) Coating & Baking (Step 1):
   - Dip each floret into the batter, shaking off excess.
   - Arrange on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Don't crowd them.
   - Bake for 20 minutes until the batter is set and starting to brown.

3) The Buffalo Sauce:
   - While baking, melt the butter in a small pan.
   - Whisk in the chili flakes (or hot sauce if available), vinegar, and paprika.

4) Glazing & Baking (Step 2):
   - Remove cauliflower from oven. Brush generously with the buffalo butter sauce.
   - Return to the oven for another 10-15 minutes until crispy and dark orange.

5) Serving:
   - Serve hot as a spicy appetizer or snack. Traditionally served with celery sticks to cool down the heat.

fields.title: Panda Style Orange Chicken
fields.description:
1) Chicken Prep:
   - Cut chicken breast into 2-3 cm cubes.
   - Toss them in flour mixed with a pinch of salt until well coated. This creates the crust.

2) Frying:
   - Heat oil in a deep pan. Fry the chicken in batches for 4-5 minutes until golden and cooked through.
   - Remove to a paper towel to drain.

3) The Orange Sauce:
   - Zest the orange (colored part of peel only) and squeeze the juice.
   - In a clean pan, combine orange juice, soy sauce, sugar (or honey), vinegar, minced garlic, and the orange zest.
   - Simmer over medium heat until the sugar dissolves and the sauce bubbles.
   - Allow it to reduce until it becomes syrupy (about 5-8 minutes).

4) Tossing:
   - Add the fried chicken pieces back into the pan with the sauce.
   - Toss quickly just to coat. Don't let them sit too long or the crust gets soggy.

5) Serving:
   - Serve immediately over steamed rice. The taste is a perfect balance of sweet, sour, and savory.

fields.title: Fudgy Chocolate Brownies
fields.description:
1) The Chocolate Base:
   - Preheat oven to 175°C. Line a square baking tin with parchment paper.
   - Melt the butter in a saucepan. Remove from heat.
   - Stir in the sugar while the butter is hot; this helps create the shiny top crust.

2) Mixing:
   - Add the cocoa powder and mix well.
   - Add eggs one by one, beating vigorously after each addition until the mixture is glossy.
   - Add vanilla sugar.

3) Dry Ingredients:
   - Sift in the flour, baking soda, and salt.
   - Fold gently with a spatula just until the white streaks disappear. DO NOT overmix, or you get cake instead of brownies.

4) Baking:
   - Pour batter into the tin and smooth the top.
   - Bake for 20-25 minutes. A toothpick inserted into the center should come out with moist crumbs attached, NOT clean (clean means dry brownies).

5) Cooling:
   - Let cool completely in the tin before slicing. They set as they cool.
   - Cut into squares and serve with a glass of milk.
